text:
`LlmCallRow`/`NewLlmCall` (llm_calls.rs): `id: i64`, `session_id: String`, `window_hash: String`, `provider: String`, `model: String`, `system_prompt: String`, `transcript_slice: String`, `raw_response: String`, `parsed_title: Option<String>`, `parsed_activity: Option<String>`, `created_at: i64` (passed in, never clock-read). [...] - **Receipt/ledger volume and privacy.** `llm_calls` stores full transcripts verbatim, forever; `receipts` has no retention; drive logs add more. Adopt X-Ray's discipline: bounded retention by bytes *and* rows, `--redact` capable end-to-end, and shareable exports gated on the trellis#161 redaction fix.
- **The value numbers are partly modeled.** The old five-trigger status path is deleted; "publishes avoided" compares against a reconstructed baseline. Label it in the output, always. Similarly, replay fidelity depends on the drive log capturing *every* seam call in order — the three graphs are mutually independent (per-surface ordering suffices), but a missed call site silently breaks trace equality; the seams ratchet is also the record-completeness ratchet. [...] - **Capsule / receipt volume + privacy.** `llm_calls` stores verbatim transcripts forever; subscription filters carry pubkeys (X-Ray flagged interest-shape as the privacy-bearing field); capsules add drive logs. Adopt bounded retention (bytes *and* rows), end-to-end `--redact`, and gate *shareable* trace export on the known Trellis audit-log redaction leak (trellis#161) — until it lands, exported capsules are local-only.
